title: EoE Instrumentality 101
wikitext:
[]: EoE Instrumentality 101             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Mar 29, 2009 9:20 pm I know there's that Angel Instrumentality vs Human Instrumentality thread but I have specific questions about EoE's Instrumentality, though some may be newbie, bear with me here. These popped into my head after chatting/debating with Eric and Legend in #egf-rpg about some things today. 1) In the coffee pot scene, it is BOTH really Asuka and Shinji, yes? I mean, so often I hear even from fans who know it's (Pre)-Instrumentality occurring, they don't think it's really Asuka there because of her cold rejection. In my opinion, that scene is meant to show both of them have completely snapped, just in their own ways. (Shinji: strangling, Asuka, rejection). Granted, I've heard the argument "it's not Asuka because the real Asuka would stop Shinji or fight back" but she DOES stop Shinji in the final scene when they're back in reality and Instrumentality has long stopped.... 2) When exactly does 3rd Impact start after the above (coffee scene)? 3) Since Yui, Lilith and Adam are Shinji's guides throughout Instrumentality and Lilith is there to give Shinji his wish, does that mean the rest of humanity was similarly guided throughout the process? We see Rei tanging people, so does everybody else have their own personal guide--such as maybe the one they loved most of all--or is Shinji a special case since he gets a "front row seat" being inside the Tree of Life while everybody else is absorbed into Lilith differently? 4) Because Shinji is given the choice to decide what he wants--life or death--although Lilith, (and to a lesser extent, Adam and Yui) are in control of 3I, if Shinji had decided not to return, does that mean the rest of humanity would be fucked? What if, say, Misato or Asuka wanted to return even if Shinji didn't? 5) Instrumentality is the joining of everybody's souls, but people do have their own individual and collective experiences as it happens apparently? That's what EoTV and to an extent EoE demonstrate.... That's all I wanted to bring up for now. If I'm asking some unanswerable questions, just say so. ;)

[]: Re: EoE Instrumentality 101             Quote Post by Itac » Sun Mar 29, 2009 9:25 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: 3) Since Yui, Lilith and Adam are Shinji's guides throughout Instrumentality and Lilith is there to give Shinji his wish, does that mean the rest of humanity was similarly guided throughout the process? We see Rei tanging people, so does everybody else have their own personal guide--such as maybe the one they loved most of all--or is Shinji a special case since he gets a "front row seat" being inside the Tree of Life while everybody else is absorbed into Lilith differently?

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sun Mar 29, 2009 9:41 pm ...

[]: Re: EoE Instrumentality 101             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 30, 2009 2:33 am 1) In the coffee pot scene, it is BOTH really Asuka and Shinji, yes?

[]: Re: EoE Instrumentality 101             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 30, 2009 9:40 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: 1) In the coffee pot scene, it is BOTH really Asuka and Shinji, yes?

[]: Re: EoE Instrumentality 101             Quote Post by VoidEater » Mon Mar 30, 2009 1:50 pm NemZ wrote: If anyone comes back with new information from this experience it will likely be about the nature of humanity as a group, not about themselves.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 30, 2009 2:04 pm Just to clarify I know that it really IS Asuka and Shinji throughout those per-instrumentality scenes, coffee pot scene included, I just wanted to get your thoughts on that. voideater wrote: deeper insight into their own psyche as well as greater perspective on the human condition, if they were paying attention...perhaps giving insight into Asuka's final words.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Mar 30, 2009 2:08 pm I actually rather suspect that in EoE the sudden onset of self after dissolving into nothingness will result in irrevocable insanity in the vast number of cases. Only those with particularly strong self-concepts have a good chance to come back, and it's still quite likely they will not put all the pieces back together quite right. Maybe even a few pieces too many or too few.

[]: Re: EoE Instrumentality 101             Quote Post by pmkava » Mon Mar 30, 2009 3:26 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: 1) In the coffee pot scene, it is BOTH really Asuka and Shinji, yes?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Mar 30, 2009 3:57 pm Instrumentality as I see it: GNR appears to Shinji, convinces him to drop his AT Field enough to let her in. He sees images/memories of the later 3I participants who are already dead (Asuka/Ritsuko/Misato all contribute) Sees an image of Asuka, snaps and strangles her. She fails to react Shinji says everyone can just die. 3I begins. Everyone else has their Fields ripped up, they are assimilated into the Black Moon, without individuality or any freedom. Shinji is isolated from this by EVA-01's AT Field. Shinji has the 3I experience Shinji realizes Instrumentality SUCKS Yui lets Shinji out of Instrumentality by destroying GNR The Black Moon is destroyed in the process, freeing everyone else in the process and allowing them to go back if they wish. Okay, that's my say. It answers the questions, I believe.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Mar 30, 2009 4:54 pm Legendary wrote: He sees images/memories of the later 3I participants who are already dead (Asuka/Ritsuko/Misato all contribute)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 30, 2009 5:04 pm Legendary wrote: Yui lets Shinji out of Instrumentality by destroying GNR

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Mon Mar 30, 2009 6:09 pm I have my own question... Didn't Yui say something about people being able to return if they could imagine themselves in their own heart? So does that mean someone could come back completely different? Like I could be tanged and come back as some Blond haired, blue eyed, asian/african mix? lol.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Mar 30, 2009 6:10 pm So long as you can remember to wire up your innards correctly too.

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Mon Mar 30, 2009 6:12 pm So Asuka and Shinji have a perfect understanding of human anatomy? I doubt that.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Mon Mar 30, 2009 6:18 pm The Point is to clearly understand yourself ,to understand that you are because you are yourself. HIP shortly show that nobody would exist if everybody merge to one beeing, The individual will lost his existance. And just maybe, Shinji and Asuka learned this experience through their expeirences in the EVAs,cause that fact that they can pilote it ,synchro rate and so on is influenced by the level of consciousness. Maybe that Level depends of the Destrudo/libido-ish mater which exist in everybody in The Eva-verse. Well, kind of drifted away with my own interpretation..

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Mon Mar 30, 2009 6:26 pm would someone who felt like a man in a woman's body or woman in a man's body come back as the opposite sex? /end fanwank Anyone else ever wonder why Shinji is the first back? He is definitely not the person with strongest sense of self, did he just skip that since he was in control of the whole thing?

[]:             Quote Post by Duke » Mon Mar 30, 2009 6:29 pm I think Yui kind of helped him realise himself.

[]:             Quote Post by Ramiel² » Mon Mar 30, 2009 6:41 pm Maybe jsut the Fact Shinji never was physically connected with other peopled tanged ,due to his entry plug,same for Asuka. Plus shinjis special place during Instrumentality who was basically some kind of observer,while Rei was next to him(kaworu too if you want) showing him how the world is if you chose this Path.. So i think At least with the fact he re-embodies starts the process of everybody to be possible to Re-tang(because the whole humanity isn't One beeing anymore and therefore imperfect). And is i got Asuka is the 1st real person who chooses herself to come back. And i think her whole appearance in NGE shows us that the most she is afraid of is be dependent of other people + Be one with someone else( oh well except Kaji *rolleyes* p.s. This Text is possbily Infacted with a big chunk of Fan-wanking,nevermind if some of my thoughts are allready stated to be different in other Threads,i try to read most of them,but to understand and remember all of it can be very stressfull...

[]: Re: EoE Instrumentality 101             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Mon Mar 30, 2009 6:48 pm 1) In the coffee pot scene, it is BOTH really Asuka and Shinji, yes?
